<<Sequences of Myths>>

The vlogger plans to visit 7 unique sites, each with its own myth. The order of visiting these sites matters. To find the number of different sequences, we calculate the factorial of 7. The result is 5040 different sequences. Each sequence represents a unique way to visit the sites.

* * *

The vlogger must consider the time spent at each site. The time spent follows a normal distribution with a mean of 3 hours. The standard deviation is 0.5 hours, which affects the spread of the distribution. The total time for 7 sites is the sum of these independent variables.

* * *

The total time spent visiting all sites is the sum of 7 independent normal variables. The mean of the total time is 21 hours. The standard deviation of the total time is calculated by taking the square root of the sum of variances. The variance for each site is 0.25, so the total variance is 1.75. The standard deviation is approximately 1.32 hours. The probability that the total time exceeds 22 hours is found by calculating the z-score and using the standard normal distribution table. The z-score is (22 - 21)/1.32 ≈ 0.76. The probability that the total time exceeds 22 hours is approximately 22.36%.
